db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');

function initializeDatabase() {
  const schema = fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'schema.sql'), 'utf-8');
  db.exec(schema);
  seedIfEmpty();
  return db;
}
```

const brewery = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM breweries WHERE id = ?').get(req.params.id);

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
    const fetchBrewery = async () => {
      setLoading(true);
      setError(null);
      try {
        const res = await fetch(`/api/breweries/${id}`);
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Brewery not found');

      } catch (err) {
        setError(err.message);
      } finally {
        setLoading(false);
      }
    };
    fetchBrewery();
  }, [id]);
```

If the fetch fails with a network error (e.g., server not running, CORS issue), the catch block sets the error. The component then shows the error state.

If the server returns a non-JSON response (e.g., HTML error page), `await res.json()` would throw a SyntaxError, which would be caught and set as the error.

So in all failure cases, the user should see the error state, not an empty page.

85:     const review = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE id = ?').get(info.lastInsertRowid);

```

If `userTrips` is an object, `.map()` would throw. But this is inside the conditional `userTrips.length > 0`, which is false for an object, so it wouldn't reach the `.map()` call.

3: const express = require('express');
4: const { db } = require('../database/db');
5: 
6: const router = express.Router();
7: 
8: /**
9:  * GET /api/beers
10:  * Query: ?brewery_id= ?style= ?q=
11:  */
12: router.get('/', (req, res, next) => {
13:   try {
14:     const { brewery_id, style, q } = req.query;
15:     const conditions = [];
16:     const params = [];
17: 
18:     if (brewery_id) {
19:       conditions.push('brewery_id = ?');
20:       params.push(brewery_id);
21:     }
22:     if (style) {
23:       conditions.push('style = ?');
24:       params.push(style);
25:     }
26:     if (q) {
27:       conditions.push('(name LIKE ? OR description LIKE ?)');
28:       params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);
29:     }
30: 
31:     const clause = conditions.length ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : '';
32:     const rows = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM beers ${clause} ORDER BY name ASC`).all(...params);
33:     res.json({ data: rows, total: rows.length });
34:   } catch (err) {
35:     next(err);
36:   }
37: });
38: 
39: /**
40:  * GET /api/beers/:id
41:  */
42: router.get('/:id', (req, res, next) => {
43:   try {
44:     const beer = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM beers WHERE id = ?').get(req.params.id);
45:     if (!beer) {
46:       const err = new Error('Beer not found');
47:       err.status = 404;
48:       throw err;
49:     }
50:     res.json({ beer });
51:   } catch (err) {
52:     next(err);
53:   }
54: });
55: 
56: /**
57:  * GET /api/breweries/:breweryId/beers
58:  */

60:   try {
61:     const { breweryId } = req.params;
62:     const rows = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM beers WHERE brewery_id = ? ORDER BY name ASC').all(breweryId);
63:     res.json({ data: rows, total: rows.length });
64:   } catch (err) {
65:     next(err);
66:   }
67: });
68: 
69: /**
70:  * GET /api/beers/styles
71:  */
72: router.get('/meta/styles', (req, res, next) => {

23:   useEffect(() => {

25:       setLoading(true);

27:       try {
28:         const res = await fetch(`/api/trips/${id}`);

53:     setSearching(true);
54:     try {

58:         setSearchResults(data.data || data);

60:     } catch (e) {

62:     } finally {

65:   };
66:

70:         searchBreweries();

74:   }, [searchQuery]);

76:   const handleSaveTrip = async () => {

82:         body: JSON.stringify(editForm),

84:       if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed to update trip');

94:   };

102:       });
103:       if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed to add stop');
104:       const data = await res.json();

115:   const handleRemoveStop = async (stopId) => {

118:       if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed to remove stop');
119:       setTrip((prev) => ({
120:         ...prev,
121:         stops: prev.stops.filter((s) => s.id !== stopId),
122:       }));

126:     }

128: 
129:   const handleMoveStop = async (stopId, direction) => {
130:     const stops = [...(trip.stops || [])];
131:     const idx = stops.findIndex((s) => s.id === stopId);
132:     if (idx < 0) return;
133:     const newIdx = idx + direction;
134:     if (newIdx < 0 || newIdx >= stops.length) return;
135:     [stops[idx], stops[newIdx]] = [stops[newIdx], stops[idx]];
136:     try {
137:       const res = await fetch(`/api/trips/${id}/stops/reorder`, {
138:         method: 'PUT',
139:         headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
140:         body: JSON.stringify({ stops: stops.map((s) => s.id) }),
141:       });
142:       if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed to reorder stops');
143:       setTrip((prev) => ({ ...prev, stops }));
144:       toast.success('Stop reordered');
145:     } catch (err) {
146:       toast.error(err.message || 'Failed to reorder stops');
147:     }
148:   };
149: 
150:   const handleDeleteTrip = async () => {
151:     try {
152:       await fetch(`/api/trips/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' });
153:       toast.success('Trip deleted');
154:       navigate('/trips');
155:     } catch (err) {

165:           <div className="h-10 bg-gray-200 rounded w-3/4" />
166:           <div className="h-64 bg-gray-200 rounded-xl" />
167:         </div>
168:       </div>
169:     );
170:   }
171:

180:           </Link>
181:         </div>
182:       </div>

185:

190:   }));
191: 
192:   return (

195:         <Link to="/trips" className="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-700 font-medium inline-flex items-center gap-1 group">

345:           <div className="bg-gray-50 rounded-xl flex items-center justify-center h-64 text-gray-500">
346:             Add stops to see the route on the map
347:           </div>
348:         )}
349:       </div>
350: 
351:       <div>
352:         <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4">Stops ({trip.stops?.length || 0})</h2>
353:         {trip.stops && trip.stops.length > 0 ? (
354:           <div className="space-y-3">
355:             {trip.stops.map((stop, index) => (
356:               <div key={stop.id} className="bg-white rounded-xl shadow-sm border border-gray-100 p-5 flex items-center justify-between hover:shadow-md transition-shadow">
357:                 <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
358:                   <div className="flex-shrink-0 w-10 h-10 bg-primary-500 text-white rounded-full flex items-center justify-center font-bold text-sm">
359:                     {index + 1}
360:                   </div>
361:                   <div>
362:                     <Link to={`/breweries/${stop.breweryId || stop.id}`} className="font-semibold text-gray-900 hover:text-primary-600 transition-colors">
363:                       {stop.name}
364:                     </Link>
365:                     <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">{stop.city}{stop.state ? `, ${stop.state}` : ''}</p>
366:                   </div>
367:                 </div>
368:                 <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
369:                   <button
370:                     onClick={() => handleMoveStop(stop.id, -1)}
371:                     disabled={index === 0}
372:                     className="p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-gray-100 disabled:opacity-30 text-gray-600 transition-colors"
373:                     title="Move up"
374:                   >
375:                     <svg className="w-4 h-4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
376:                       <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" strokeWidth={2} d="M5 15l7-7 7 7" />
377:                     </svg>
378:                   </button>
379:                   <button
380:                     onClick={() => handleMoveStop(stop.id, 1)}
381:                     disabled={index === trip.stops.length - 1}
382:                     className="p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-gray-100 disabled:opacity-30 text-gray-600 transition-colors"
383:                     title="Move down"
384:                   >
385:                     <svg className="w-4 h-4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
386:                       <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" strokeWidth={2} d="M19 9l-7 7-7-7" />
387:                     </svg>
388:                   </button>
389:                   <button
390:                     onClick={() => handleRemoveStop(stop.id)}
391:                     className="p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-red-50 text-red-500 transition-colors"
392:                     title="Remove stop"
393:                   >
394:                     <svg className="w-4 h-4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
395:                       <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" strokeWidth={2} d="M19 7l-.867 12.142A2 2 0 0116.138 21H7.862a2 2 0 01-1.995-1.858L5 7m5 4v6m4-6v6m1-10V4a1 1 0 00-1-1h-4a1 1 0 00-1 1v3M4 7h16" />
396:                     </svg>
397:                   </button>
398:                 </div>
399:               </div>
400:             ))}
401:           </div>
402:         ) : (
403:           <div className="text-center py-12 bg-white rounded-xl border border-gray-100">
404:             <div className="text-4xl mb-3">📍</div>
405:             <p className="text-gray-500">No stops yet. Add your first brewery!</p>
406:           </div>
407:         )}
408:       </div>
409: 
410:       {showDeleteConfirm && (

429:                 Delete Trip

## Assistant (Orchestrator · Auto Free · 352.1s)

_Thinking:_

Now I have a clear picture of all the API response formats and which client-side patterns are wrong:

**API response formats:**
1. `GET /api/breweries/:id` → `{ brewery: {...} }` ✓
2. `GET /api/reviews/brewery/:id` → `{ data: [...], averageRating, count }` ✗ (component expects `data.reviews`)
3. `GET /api/trips` → `{ data: [...] }` ✗ (component expects `data.trips`)
4. `GET /api/beers/:id` → `{ beer: {...} }` ✓
5. `GET /api/trips/:id` → `{ ...trip, stops }` ✓ (but component uses `data.trip`)
6. `GET /api/breweries` → `{ data: [...], page, limit, total, totalPages }` ✓ (component uses `data.data`)
7. `GET /api/challenges/my-challenges` → `{ data: [...], total }` ✓ (component uses `data.data`)

**Wrong patterns in client code:**
1. `BreweryDetail.jsx` line 48: `setReviews(data.reviews || data)` should be `setReviews(data.data || [])`
2. `BreweryDetail.jsx` line 73: `setUserTrips(data.trips || data)` should be `setUserTrips(data.data || [])`
3. `BreweryDetail.jsx` line 139: `setReviews(data.reviews || data)` should be `setReviews(data.data || [])`
4. `Trips.jsx` line 20: `setTrips(data.trips || data)` should be `setTrips(data.data || [])`
5. `TripDetail.jsx` line 31: `const tripData = data.trip || data` should be `const tripData = data` (API returns `{ ...trip, stops }`, not `{ trip }`)
6. `TripDetail.jsx` line 86: `setTrip(data.trip || data)` should be `setTrip(data)` 
7. `TripDetail.jsx` line 105: `setTrip(data.trip || data)` should be `setTrip(data)`

The most critical one causing the empty page is #1 - the reviews fetch in BreweryDetail. When `setReviews(data.reviews || data)` runs with the reviews API response, `data.reviews` is undefined, so it falls back to `data` (the whole object `{ data: [...], averageRating, count }`). Then when the component tries to render `reviews.map(...)`, it crashes because `reviews` is an object, not an array.

Let me fix all of these issues. I'll also fix the TripDetail issues since they have similar problems.

For BreweryDetail.jsx, the critical fixes are:
1. Line 48: `setReviews(data.reviews || data)` → `setReviews(data.data || [])`
2. Line 73: `setUserTrips(data.trips || data)` → `setUserTrips(data.data || [])`
3. Line 139: `setReviews(data.reviews || data)` → `setReviews(data.data || [])`

For Trips.jsx:
4. Line 20: `setTrips(data.trips || data)` → `setTrips(data.data || [])`

For TripDetail.jsx:
5. Line 31: `const tripData = data.trip || data` → `const tripData = data`
6. Line 86: `setTrip(data.trip || data)` → `setTrip(data)`
7. Line 105: `setTrip(data.trip || data)` → `setTrip(data)`

Let me also check line 58 in TripDetail.jsx:
```jsx
setSearchResults(data.data || data);
```
The search is against `/api/breweries?q=...` which returns `{ data: [...], page, limit, total, totalPages }`. So `data.data || data` is actually correct here! `data.data` is the array of breweries. This one is fine.

Let me also check the BeersSection in BreweryDetail.jsx (lines 380-453). It uses:

setBeers(data.data || []);
```
The beers API returns `{ data: [...], total }`, so this is correct.

OK, let me now apply all the fixes. I'll update BreweryDetail.jsx, Trips.jsx, and TripDetail.jsx.

I've identified the root cause of the empty page and several related API-response mismatches across the codebase.

**Primary bug:** `BreweryDetail.jsx` line 48 reads `data.reviews` from the reviews API, but the API returns `{ data: [...], averageRating, count }`. The fallback stores the whole response object in `reviews`, and `reviews.map(...)` later throws `TypeError: reviews.map is not a function`, crashing React into a blank page.

There are also similar mismatches in `Trips.jsx` and `TripDetail.jsx`.

Replace the raw fetch in `fetchTrips` (lines 16-20):\n```jsx\n      try {\n        const res = await fetch('/api/trips');\n        if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch trips');\n        const data = await res.json();\n        setTrips(data.data || []);\n```\nwith:\n```jsx\n      try {\n        const { data } = await api.get('/api/trips');\n        setTrips(data.data || []);\n```\n\nReturn the modified file content to confirm.",

}

```jsx
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import { useParams, Link } from 'react-router-dom';
import Map from '../components/Map';
import ReviewStars from '../components/ReviewStars';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import toast from 'react-hot-toast';
import api from '../utils/api';

export default function BreweryDetail() {
  const { id } = useParams();
  const [brewery, setBrewery] = useState(null);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  const [error, setError] = useState(null);
  const [reviews, setReviews] = useState([]);
  const [reviewsLoading, setReviewsLoading] = useState(true);
  const [isFavorite, setIsFavorite] = useState(false);
  const [favoriteLoading, setFavoriteLoading] = useState(false);
  const [userTrips, setUserTrips] = useState([]);
  const [showTripDropdown, setShowTripDropdown] = useState(false);
  const [reviewForm, setReviewForm] = useState({ rating: 5, comment: '' });
  const [submittingReview, setSubmittingReview] = useState(false);
  const { user } = useAuth();

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchBrewery = async () => {
      setLoading(true);
      setError(null);
      try {
        const res = await fetch(`/api/breweries/${id}`);
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Brewery not found');
        const data = await res.json();
        setBrewery(data.brewery || data);
      } catch (err) {
        setError(err.message);
      } finally {
        setLoading(false);
      }
    };
    fetchBrewery();
  }, [id]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchReviews = async () => {
      setReviewsLoading(true);
      try {
        const res = await fetch(`/api/reviews/brewery/${id}`);
        if (res.ok) {
          const data = await res.json();
          setReviews(data.data || []);
        }
      } catch (e) {
        console.error('Failed to fetch reviews:', e);
      } finally {
        setReviewsLoading(false);
      }
    };
    if (id) fetchReviews();
  }, [id]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const checkFavorite = async () => {
      try {
        const res = await api.get(`/api/favorites/${id}`);
        setIsFavorite(res.ok);
      } catch (e) {
        setIsFavorite(false);
      }
    };
    const fetchUserTrips = async () => {
      try {
        const { data } = await api.get('/api/trips');
        setUserTrips(data.data || []);
      } catch (e) {
        console.error('Failed to fetch trips:', e);
      }
    };
    if (user) {
      checkFavorite();
      fetchUserTrips();
    }
  }, [id, user]);

  const toggleFavorite = async () => {
    if (!user) {
      toast.error('Please login to add favorites');
      return;
    }
    setFavoriteLoading(true);
    try {
      if (isFavorite) {
        await api.delete(`/api/favorites/${id}`);
        setIsFavorite(false);
        toast.success('Removed from favorites');
      } else {
        await api.post(`/api/favorites/${id}`);
        setIsFavorite(true);
        toast.success('Added to favorites');
      }
    } catch (err) {
      toast.error(err.response?.data?.error || err.message || 'Failed to update favorites');
    } finally {
      setFavoriteLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const handleAddToTrip = async (tripId) => {
    try {
      await api.post(`/api/trips/${tripId}/stops`, {
        breweryId: id,
        name: brewery.name,
        city: brewery.city,
        state: brewery.state,
        latitude: brewery.latitude,
        longitude: brewery.longitude,
      });
      toast.success('Brewery added to trip!');
      setShowTripDropdown(false);
    } catch (err) {
      toast.error(err.response?.data?.error || err.message || 'Failed to add to trip');
    }
  };

  const handleSubmitReview = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (submittingReview) return;
    setSubmittingReview(true);
    try {
      await api.post('/api/reviews', {
        breweryId: id,
        rating: reviewForm.rating,
        comment: reviewForm.comment,
      });
      toast.success('Review submitted!');
      setReviewForm({ rating: 5, comment: '' });
      const { data } = await api.get(`/api/reviews/brewery/${id}`);
      setReviews(data.data || []);
    } catch (err) {
      toast.error(err.response?.data?.error || err.message || 'Failed to submit review');
    } finally {
      setSubmittingReview(false);
    }
  };

  if (loading) {
    return (
      <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 py-8">
        <div className="animate-pulse space-y-4">
          <div className="h-6 bg-gray-200 rounded w-32" />
          <div className="h-10 bg-gray-200 rounded w-3/4" />
          <div className="h-64 bg-gray-200 rounded-xl" />
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }

  if (error || !brewery) {
    return (
      <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 py-8">
        <div className="text-center py-20">
          <div className="text-6xl mb-4">😕</div>
          <p className="text-gray-500 text-lg mb-4">{error || 'Brewery not found'}</p>
          <Link to="/breweries" className="text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-700 font-medium">
            Back to Breweries
          </Link>
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }

  const avgRating = reviews.length > 0
    ? (reviews.reduce((sum, r) => sum + r.rating, 0) / reviews.length).toFixed(1)
    : (brewery.rating || 'N/A');

  return (
    <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 py-8 animate-fade-in">
      <Link to="/breweries" className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 text-primary-600 hover:text-primary-700 font-medium mb-6 group">
        <svg className="w-4 h-4 group-hover:-translate-x-1 transition-transform" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
          <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" strokeWidth={2} d="M15 19l-7-7 7-7" />
        </svg>
        Back to Breweries
      </Link>

      <div className="bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-sm border border-gray-100 overflow-hidden mb-8">
        <div className="relative h-72 md:h-80 bg-gradient-to-br from-amber-100 via-orange-50 to-yellow-50 flex items-center justify-center">
          <span className="text-8xl">🍺</span>
          {brewery.type && (
            <span className="absolute top-6 left-6 px-3 py-1.5 bg-white/90 backdrop-blur-sm text-amber-800 rounded-full text-sm font-semibold shadow-sm">
              {brewery.type}
            </span>
          )}
          {brewery.country && brewery.country !== 'USA' && (
            <span className="absolute top-6 right-6 px-3 py-1.5 bg-primary-500 text-white rounded-full text-sm font-semibold shadow-sm">
              {brewery.country}
            </span>
          )}
        </div>

        <div className="p-6 md:p-8">
          <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row md:items-start md:justify-between mb-6 gap-4">
            <div>
              <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-2">{brewery.name}</h1>
              <p className="text-gray-600 flex items-center gap-1.5">
                <svg className="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
                  <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" strokeWidth={2} d="M17.657 16.657L13.414 20.9a1.998 1.998 0 01-2.827 0l-4.244-4.243A8 8 0 1117.657 16.657z" />
                  <circle cx="12" cy="11" r="1.5" fill="currentColor" />
                </svg>
                {brewery.city}{brewery.state ? `, ${brewery.state}` : ''}
              </p>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center gap-2 bg-amber-50 px-4 py-2 rounded-xl">
              <ReviewStars rating={typeof avgRating === 'number' ? avgRating : 0} />
              <span className="text-sm text-gray-600 font-medium">({reviews.length} reviews)</span>
            </div>
          </div>

          {brewery.description && (
            <p className="text-gray-700 leading-relaxed mb-8 text-lg">{brewery.description}</p>
          )}

          <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-4 mb-8">
            {brewery.website && (
              <a href={brewery.website} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" className="bg-gray-50 rounded-xl p-4 hover:bg-gray-100 transition-colors group">
                <h3 className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-500 mb-1">Website</h3>
                <p className="text-primary-600 font-medium truncate group-hover:underline">{brewery.website}</p>
              </a>
            )}
            {brewery.phone && (
              <div className="bg-gray-50 rounded-xl p-4">
                <h3 className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-500 mb-1">Phone</h3>
                <p className="text-gray-900 font-medium">{brewery.phone}</p>
              </div>
            )}
            <div className="bg-gray-50 rounded-xl p-4">
              <h3 className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-500 mb-1">Address</h3>
              <p className="text-gray-900">{brewery.address || `${brewery.city}, ${brewery.state}`}</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-3 mb-8">
            {user && (
              <button
                onClick={toggleFavorite}
                disabled={favoriteLoading}
                className={`px-5 py-2.5 rounded-xl font-medium transition-all ${
                  isFavorite
                    ? 'bg-red-100 text-red-700 hover:bg-red-200'
                    : 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700 hover
</task_result>
</task>

## Assistant (Orchestrator · Auto Free · 11.8s)

_Thinking:_

The user said "continue" - they want me to summarize what was done and what the current state is. Let me provide a clear summary of all the fixes made.

Let me also check if there are any remaining issues I should address. The main bugs were:
1. ChallengeRoulette spin not working - FIXED
2. BreweryDetail empty page for /breweries/13 - FIXED
3. Related API response mismatches - FIXED
4. Raw fetch auth issues - FIXED

I should summarize everything clearly.

## Summary of fixes applied

### 1. `/challenges` — Roulette spin broken
**Root cause:** Two bugs in `ChallengeRoulette.jsx` / `Challenges.jsx`:
- **Auth:** `handleAccept`, `fetchMyChallenges`, and `CompleteChallengeButton` used raw `fetch()` instead of the authenticated `api` instance, so JWT Bearer tokens were never sent → 401 on every challenge action.
- **Animation race:** `onSpin()` fired immediately on click while the 2-second animation ran independently, causing flickering or the wrong challenge to display.

**Fix:** Rewrote `ChallengeRoulette` to run the animation first, then call `onSpin()` only after completion. Switched all challenge-related fetches to the `api` instance with `resultChallenge` state to hold the server-returned challenge.

---

### 2. `/breweries/13` — Empty page
**Root cause:** In `BreweryDetail.jsx`, the reviews fetch did `setReviews(data.reviews || data)`, but the reviews API returns `{ data: [...], averageRating, count }`. Since `data.reviews` was `undefined`, the entire response object `{ data: [...], ... }` was stored as `reviews`. When React later tried `reviews.map(...)`, it threw `TypeError: reviews.map is not a function`, crashing the render into a blank page.

**Fix:** Changed all reviews data assignments to `setReviews(data.data || [])`.

---

### 3. Related API response mismatches found and fixed
While investigating, I found the same `data.xxx || data` pattern was wrong in several other files:

| File | Line | Wrong | Correct |
|------|------|-------|---------|
| `BreweryDetail.jsx` | 73 | `setUserTrips(data.trips \|\| data)` | `setUserTrips(data.data \|\| [])` |
| `BreweryDetail.jsx` | 139 | `setReviews(data.reviews \|\| data)` | `setReviews(data.data \|\| [])` |
| `Trips.jsx` | 20 | `setTrips(data.trips \|\| data)` | `setTrips(data.data \|\| [])` |
| `TripDetail.jsx` | 86 | `setTrip(data.trip \|\| data)` | `setTrip(data.trip \|\| data)` (PUT returns `{ trip }`) |
| `TripDetail.jsx` | 105 | `setTrip(data.trip \|\| data)` | `setTrip(prev => ({ ...prev, stops: [...(prev.stops \|\| []), data.stop] }))` (POST returns `{ stop }`) |

---

### 4. Auth token missing on raw `fetch` calls
All trip/favorite/review mutation endpoints require JWT auth middleware (`router.use(auth)`), but `BreweryDetail.jsx` and `Trips.jsx` used raw `fetch()` which doesn't attach the Bearer token. This would cause 401 errors for logged-in users.

**Fix:** Replaced raw `fetch` with the authenticated `api` instance (`api.get`, `api.post`, `api.delete`) in:
- `BreweryDetail.jsx`: `checkFavorite`, `fetchUserTrips`, `toggleFavorite`, `handleAddToTrip`, `handleSubmitReview`
- `Trips.jsx`: `fetchTrips`

---

<skill_content name="deploy-to-vercel">
# Skill: deploy-to-vercel

# Deploy to Vercel

Deploy any project to Vercel. **Always deploy as preview** (not production) unless the user explicitly asks for production.

The goal is to get the user into the best long-term setup: their project linked to Vercel with git-push deploys. Every method below tries to move the user closer to that state.

## Step 1: Gather Project State

Run all four checks before deciding which method to use:

```bash
# 1. Check for a git remote
git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null

# 2. Check if locally linked to a Vercel project (either file means linked)
cat .vercel/project.json 2>/dev/null || cat .vercel/repo.json 2>/dev/null

# 3. Check if the Vercel CLI is installed and authenticated
vercel whoami 2>/dev/null

# 4. List available teams (if authenticated)
vercel teams list --format json 2>/dev/null
```

### Team selection

If the user belongs to multiple teams, present all available team slugs as a bulleted list and ask which one to deploy to. Once the user picks a team, proceed immediately to the next step — do not ask for additional confirmation.

Pass the team slug via `--scope` on all subsequent CLI commands (`vercel deploy`, `vercel link`, `vercel inspect`, etc.):

```bash
vercel deploy [path] -y --no-wait --scope <team-slug>
```

If the project is already linked (`.vercel/project.json` or `.vercel/repo.json` exists), the `orgId` in those files determines the team — no need to ask again. If there is only one team (or just a personal account), skip the prompt and use it directly.

**About the `.vercel/` directory:** A linked project has either:
- `.vercel/project.json` — created by `vercel link` (single project linking). Contains `projectId` and `orgId`.
- `.vercel/repo.json` — created by `vercel link --repo` (repo-based linking). Contains `orgId`, `remoteName`, and a `projects` array mapping directories to Vercel project IDs.

Either file means the project is linked. Check for both.

**Do NOT** use `vercel project inspect`, `vercel ls`, or `vercel link` to detect state in an unlinked directory — without a `.vercel/` config, they will interactively prompt (or with `--yes`, silently link as a side-effect). Only `vercel whoami` is safe to run anywhere.

## Step 2: Choose a Deploy Method

### Linked (`.vercel/` exists) + has git remote → Git Push

This is the ideal state. The project is linked and has git integration.

1. **Ask the user before pushing.** Never push without explicit approval:
   ```
   This project is connected to Vercel via git. I can commit and push to
   trigger a deployment. Want me to proceed?
   ```

2. **Commit and push:**
   ```bash
   git add .
   git commit -m "deploy: <description of changes>"
   git push
   ```
   Vercel automatically builds from the push. Non-production branches get preview deployments; the production branch (usually `main`) gets a production deployment.

3. **Retrieve the preview URL.** If the CLI is authenticated:
   ```bash
   sleep 5
   vercel ls --format json
   ```
   The JSON output has a `deployments` array. Find the latest entry — its `url` field is the preview URL.

   If the CLI is not authenticated, tell the user to check the Vercel dashboard or the commit status checks on their git provider for the preview URL.

---

### Linked (`.vercel/` exists) + no git remote → `vercel deploy`

The project is linked but there's no git repo. Deploy directly with the CLI.

```bash
vercel deploy [path] -y --no-wait
```

Use `--no-wait` so the CLI returns immediately with the deployment URL instead of blocking until the build finishes (builds can take a while). Then check on the deployment status with:

```bash
vercel inspect <deployment-url>
```

For production deploys (only if user explicitly asks):
```bash
vercel deploy [path] --prod -y --no-wait
```

---

### Not linked + CLI is authenticated → Link first, then deploy

The CLI is working but the project isn't linked yet. This is the opportunity to get the user into the best state.

1. **Ask the user which team to deploy to.** Present the team slugs from Step 1 as a bulleted list. If there's only one team (or just a personal account), skip this step.

2. **Once a team is selected, proceed directly to linking.** Tell the user what will happen but do not ask for separate confirmation:
   ```
   Linking this project to <team name> on Vercel. This will create a Vercel
   project to deploy to and enable automatic deployments on future git pushes.
   ```

3. **If a git remote exists**, use repo-based linking with the selected team scope:
   ```bash
   vercel link --repo --scope <team-slug>
   ```
   This reads the git remote URL and matches it to existing Vercel projects that deploy from that repo. It creates `.vercel/repo.json`. This is much more reliable than `vercel link` (without `--repo`), which tries to match by directory name and often fails when the local folder and Vercel project are named differently.

   **If there is no git remote**, fall back to standard linking:
   ```bash
   vercel link --scope <team-slug>
   ```
   This prompts the user to select or create a project. It creates `.vercel/project.json`.

4. **Then deploy using the best available method:**
   - If a git remote exists → commit and push (see git push method above)
   - If no git remote → `vercel deploy [path] -y --no-wait --scope <team-slug>`, then `vercel inspect <url>` to check status

---

### Not linked + CLI not authenticated → Install, auth, link, deploy

The Vercel CLI isn't set up at all.

1. **Install the CLI (if not already installed):**
   ```bash
   npm install -g vercel
   ```

2. **Authenticate:**
   ```bash
   vercel login
   ```
   The user completes authentication in their browser. If interactive authentication is unavailable or fails, stop and explain that an authenticated Vercel CLI session is required. Do not package or upload the project through another service.

3. **Ask which team to deploy to** — present team slugs from `vercel teams list --format json` as a bulleted list. If only one team / personal account, skip. Once selected, proceed immediately.

4. **Link the project** with the selected team scope (use `--repo` if a git remote exists, plain `vercel link` otherwise):
   ```bash
   vercel link --repo --scope <team-slug>   # if git remote exists
   vercel link --scope <team-slug>          # if no git remote
   ```

5. **Deploy** using the best available method (git push if remote exists, otherwise `vercel deploy -y --no-wait --scope <team-slug>`, then `vercel inspect <url>` to check status).

---

### Authentication unavailable → Stop

Do not deploy without an authenticated Vercel CLI session. Do not run bundled fallback scripts, send the project to an unauthenticated deployment endpoint, or upload an archive through another service.

Tell the user that deployment stopped because Vercel authentication is required. They can run `vercel login` and retry after authentication succeeds.

---

## Output

Always show the user the deployment URL.

- **Git push:** Use `vercel ls --format json` to find the preview URL. If the CLI isn't authenticated, tell the user to check the Vercel dashboard or commit status checks.
- **CLI deploy:** Show the URL returned by `vercel deploy --no-wait`. Use `vercel inspect <url>` to check build status and report it to the user.

**Do not** curl or fetch the deployed URL to verify it works. Just return the link.

---

## Troubleshooting

### Network Access Error

If deployment fails because network access is unavailable, report the failure and stop. Do not route the project through a different deployment endpoint.

### CLI Auth Failure

If `vercel login` or `vercel deploy` fails with authentication errors, report that an authenticated Vercel CLI session is required and stop.

I can't execute shell commands in this environment, but here are the exact steps you need to run to create the GitHub repo, link it to Vercel, and deploy.

### Step 1: Initialize git and create GitHub repo

```bash
cd /home/anton/projects/beer-road

# Initialize git repo
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit: Beer Road Save full-stack app"

# Create GitHub repo and push (replace <your-username> with your GitHub username)
gh repo create beer-road-save --public --source=. --remote=origin --push
```

### Step 2: Link to Vercel

```bash
# Install Vercel CLI if not already installed
npm install -g vercel

# Authenticate
vercel login

# Link the project (this creates .vercel/project.json)
vercel link --repo
```

When prompted, select the GitHub repo you just created.

### Step 3: Deploy

```bash
# Deploy to preview
vercel deploy -y --no-wait
```

### Important: Backend API note

Your current `vercel.json` only builds the **frontend** as static files:

```json
{
  "builds": [{ "src": "client/package.json", "use": "@vercel/static-build" }],
  "routes": [{ "src": "/(.*)", "dest": "/client/$1" }]
}
```

This means the Express backend (`/api/breweries`, `/api/trips`, etc.) is **not deployed** — only the Vite build output is. For a full-stack deployment on Vercel, you'd need to either:

1. **Convert backend to Vercel serverless functions** — move Express routes into `/api/` files
2. **Deploy backend separately** — e.g., Render, Railway, Fly.io, or a VPS
3. **Use a full-stack framework** — Next.js with API routes instead of separate Express server

The current frontend build will work, but API calls will fail because there's no backend running.

**Tool: task**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "description": "Fix DB path for Vercel serverless",
  "prompt": "Update `/home/anton/projects/beer-road/server/database/db.js` to work with Vercel's ephemeral filesystem.\n\nVercel serverless functions have a read-only filesystem except for `/tmp`. The SQLite database must be stored in `/tmp` on Vercel, but for local development the current path (`__dirname/beer-road.db`) is fine.\n\nVercel sets the environment variable `VERCEL=1`.\n\nMake these changes:\n\n1. Update the DB_PATH logic to use `/tmp/beer-road.db` when running on Vercel:\n```js\n// Database file lives next to this module. Override with the DB_PATH env var.\n// On Vercel serverless, use /tmp because the rest of the filesystem is read-only.\nconst DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH || (process.env.VERCEL ? '/tmp/beer-road.db' : path.join(__dirname, 'beer-road.db'));\n```\n\n2. Keep everything else the same.\n\nReturn the full modified file content.",
  "subagent_type": "general"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<task id="ses_09affe367ffepRW77lpYPJhpsJ" state="completed">
<task_result>
```js
'use strict';

const path = require('path');
const fs = require('fs');
const Database = require('better-sqlite3');

// Database file lives next to this module. Override with the DB_PATH env var.
// On Vercel serverless, use /tmp because the rest of the filesystem is read-only.
const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH || (process.env.VERCEL ? '/tmp/beer-road.db' : path.join(__dirname, 'beer-road.db'));

const db = new Database(DB_PATH);
// WAL gives better concurrency for reads while writes happen.
db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
// Enforce FK constraints so deletes/inserts stay consistent.
db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');

/**
 * Create the schema (idempotent) and seed sample data on first run.
 * Called once at startup before the HTTP server starts accepting traffic.
 */
function initializeDatabase() {
  const schema = fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'schema.sql'), 'utf-8');
  db.exec(schema);
  seedIfEmpty();
  return db;
}

/**
 * Insert seed breweries only when the breweries table is empty, so the
 * database can be safely re-initialized without duplicating rows.
 */
function seedIfEmpty() {
  const { count: breweries } = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM breweries').get();
  const { count: beers } = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM beers').get();
  if (breweries > 0 && beers > 0) return;

  const seed = fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'seed.sql'), 'utf-8');
  // Wrap in a transaction so a partial failure leaves the DB untouched.
  // INSERT OR IGNORE keeps re-seeding idempotent (rows are no-ops if present).
  const tx = db.transaction(() => db.exec(seed));
  tx();
}

module.exports = { db, initializeDatabase, DB_PATH };
```
</task_result>
</task>
```

## Assistant (Orchestrator · Auto Free · 15.5s)

_Thinking:_

Now let me provide the deployment instructions clearly.

All backend files are ready for Vercel serverless deployment. Here are the exact steps:

### Step 1: Initialize git and create GitHub repo

```bash
cd /home/anton/projects/beer-road

# Initialize git
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit: Beer Road Save full-stack app"

# Create GitHub repo (replace <your-username> with your actual GitHub username)
gh repo create beer-road-save --public --source=. --remote=origin --push
```

### Step 2: Install dependencies

```bash
# Install server dependencies (including serverless-http)
cd /home/anton/projects/beer-road/server
npm install

# Go back to root
cd /home/anton/projects/beer-road
```

### Step 3: Install Vercel CLI and login

```bash
npm install -g vercel
vercel login
```

This opens a browser for authentication. Complete the login in the browser.

### Step 4: Deploy to Vercel

```bash
cd /home/anton/projects/beer-road
vercel deploy -y --no-wait
```

The `--no-wait` flag returns immediately with the deployment URL. You can check build status with:

```bash
vercel inspect <deployment-url>
```

### Step 5: Verify deployment

Open the returned preview URL and test:
- Homepage loads
- `/breweries` page works
- `/breweries/13` detail page works
- `/challenges` roulette spin works

---

### Important notes

**SQLite on Vercel:** The database uses `/tmp/beer-road.db` on Vercel, which is **ephemeral** — data resets on every new deployment and between cold starts. This is fine for demos/prototypes. For production, replace SQLite with a cloud database like Neon Postgres, Supabase, or Vercel Postgres.

**Local development unchanged:** The local dev setup (`npm run dev`) still works the same way — Vite proxies `/api` to the Express server on port 3001 with the SQLite DB in the project directory.

## Assistant (Orchestrator · Auto Free · 14.6s)

_Thinking:_

The user wants to use Neon Postgres instead of SQLite. This is a significant change - I need to:

1. Replace better-sqlite3 with a Postgres client (pg or postgres.js)
2. Rewrite all database queries from SQLite syntax to Postgres syntax
3. Update the database initialization and seeding
4. Handle the connection properly for Vercel serverless

Let me first understand the current database schema and all the queries used across the codebase, then plan the migration.

Key differences between SQLite and Postgres:
- SQLite: `SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM table` → Postgres: same
- SQLite: `INSERT ... VALUES ...` → Postgres: same, but returning `lastInsertRowid` becomes `RETURNING id`
- SQLite: `AUTOINCREMENT` → Postgres: `SERIAL` or `GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY`
- SQLite: `datetime('now')` → Postgres: `now()` or `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`
- SQLite: `COALESCE(?, column)` for updates → same in Postgres
- SQLite: `exec(schema)` for creating tables → same in Postgres
- SQLite: UNIQUE constraints → same
- SQLite: `changes` property on prepared statements → use `rowCount` in Postgres

Let me read all the route files and database files to understand the full scope.

<path>/home/anton/projects/beer-road/server/database/db.js</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: 'use strict';
2: 
3: const path = require('path');
4: const fs = require('fs');
5: const Database = require('better-sqlite3');
6: 
7: // Database file lives next to this module. Override with the DB_PATH env var.
8: // On Vercel serverless, use /tmp because the rest of the filesystem is read-only.
9: const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH || (process.env.VERCEL ? '/tmp/beer-road.db' : path.join(__dirname, 'beer-road.db'));
10: 
11: const db = new Database(DB_PATH);
12: // WAL gives better concurrency for reads while writes happen.
13: db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
14: // Enforce FK constraints so deletes/inserts stay consistent.
15: db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
16: 
17: /**
18:  * Create the schema (idempotent) and seed sample data on first run.
19:  * Called once at startup before the HTTP server starts accepting traffic.
20:  */
21: function initializeDatabase() {
22:   const schema = fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'schema.sql'), 'utf-8');
23:   db.exec(schema);
24:   seedIfEmpty();
25:   return db;
26: }
27: 
28: /**
29:  * Insert seed breweries only when the breweries table is empty, so the
30:  * database can be safely re-initialized without duplicating rows.
31:  */
32: function seedIfEmpty() {
33:   const { count: breweries } = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM breweries').get();
34:   const { count: beers } = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM beers').get();
35:   if (breweries > 0 && beers > 0) return;
36: 
37:   const seed = fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'seed.sql'), 'utf-8');
38:   // Wrap in a transaction so a partial failure leaves the DB untouched.
39:   // INSERT OR IGNORE keeps re-seeding idempotent (rows are no-ops if present).
40:   const tx = db.transaction(() => db.exec(seed));
41:   tx();
42: }
43: 
44: module.exports = { db, initializeDatabase, DB_PATH };

(End of file - total 44 lines)
</content>
```

1: 'use strict';
2: 
3: const express = require('express');
4: const bcrypt = require('bcrypt');
5: const { db } = require('../database/db');
6: const { auth, generateToken } = require('../middleware/auth');
7: 
8: const router = express.Router();
9: 
10: /**
11:  * POST /api/auth/register
12:  * Body: { email, password, name }
13:  */
14: router.post('/register', (req, res, next) => {
15:   try {
16:     const { email, password, name } = req.body;
17:     if (!email || !password) {
18:       const err = new Error('Email and password are required');
19:       err.status = 400;
20:       throw err;
21:     }
22:     if (String(password).length < 6) {
23:       const err = new Error('Password must be at least 6 characters');
24:       err.status = 400;
25:       throw err;
26:     }
27: 
28:     const normalizedEmail = String(email).toLowerCase().trim();
29:     const existing = db.prepare('SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?').get(normalizedEmail);
30:     if (existing) {
31:       const err = new Error('Email already registered');
32:       err.status = 409;
33:       throw err;
34:     }
35: 
36:     const passwordHash = bcrypt.hashSync(password, 10);
37:     const info = db
38:       .prepare('INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)')
39:       .run(normalizedEmail, passwordHash, name ? String(name).trim() : null);
40: 
41:     const user = db
42:       .prepare('SELECT id, email, name, created_at FROM users WHERE id = ?')
43:       .get(info.lastInsertRowid);
44: 
45:     res.status(201).json({ token: generateToken(user), user });
46:   } catch (err) {
47:     next(err);
48:   }
49: });
50: 
51: /**
52:  * POST /api/auth/login
53:  * Body: { email, password }
54:  */
55: router.post('/login', (req, res, next) => {
56:   try {
57:     const { email, password } = req.body;
58:     if (!email || !password) {
59:       const err = new Error('Email and password are required');
60:       err.status = 400;
61:       throw err;
62:     }
63: 
64:     const user = db
65:       .prepare('SELECT id, email, name, password_hash FROM users WHERE email = ?')
66:       .get(String(email).toLowerCase().trim());
67:     if (!user || !bcrypt.compareSync(password, user.password_hash)) {
68:       const err = new Error('Invalid credentials');
69:       err.status = 401;
70:       throw err;
71:     }
72: 
73:     const safeUser = { id: user.id, email: user.email, name: user.name };
74:     res.json({ token: generateToken(safeUser), user: safeUser });
75:   } catch (err) {
76:     next(err);
77:   }
78: });
79: 
80: /**
81:  * GET /api/auth/me
82:  * Returns the authenticated user's profile.
83:  */
84: router.get('/me', auth, (req, res, next) => {
85:   try {
86:     const user = db
87:       .prepare('SELECT id, email, name, created_at FROM users WHERE id = ?')
88:       .get(req.user.id);
89:     if (!user) {
90:       const err = new Error('User not found');
91:       err.status = 404;
92:       throw err;
93:     }
94:     res.json({ user });
95:   } catch (err) {
96:     next(err);
97:   }
98: });
99: 
100: module.exports = router;

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: 'use strict';
2: 
3: const express = require('express');
4: const { db } = require('../database/db');
5: const { auth } = require('../middleware/auth');
6: 
7: const router = express.Router();
8: 
9: /**
10:  * Build a parameterized WHERE clause from the provided filters.
11:  * Returns { clause, params } where clause is "" or "WHERE ...".
12:  */
13: function buildBreweryFilters({ city, state, type, country, q }) {
14:   const conditions = [];
15:   const params = [];
16: 
17:   if (city) {
18:     conditions.push('city LIKE ?');
19:     params.push(`%${city}%`);
20:   }
21:   if (state) {
22:     conditions.push('state LIKE ?');
23:     params.push(`%${state}%`);
24:   }
25:   if (type) {
26:     conditions.push('type = ?');
27:     params.push(type);
28:   }
29:   if (country) {
30:     conditions.push('country = ?');
31:     params.push(country);
32:   }
33:   if (q) {
34:     // Free-text search across the most useful columns.
35:     conditions.push('(name LIKE ? OR description LIKE ? OR city LIKE ? OR address LIKE ?)');
36:     params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`, `%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);
37:   }
38: 
39:   const clause = conditions.length ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : '';
40:   return { clause, params };
41: }
42: 
43: /**
44:  * GET /api/breweries/filters/cities
45:  * Returns distinct list of cities for filter dropdowns.
46:  */
47: router.get('/filters/cities', (req, res, next) => {
48:   try {
49:     const cities = db.prepare('SELECT DISTINCT city FROM breweries WHERE city IS NOT NULL ORDER BY city ASC').all();
50:     res.json(cities.map((row) => row.city));
51:   } catch (err) {
52:     next(err);
53:   }
54: });
55: 
56: /**
57:  * GET /api/breweries/filters/states
58:  * Returns distinct list of states for filter dropdowns.
59:  */
60: router.get('/filters/states', (req, res, next) => {
61:   try {
62:     const states = db.prepare('SELECT DISTINCT state FROM breweries WHERE state IS NOT NULL ORDER BY state ASC').all();
63:     res.json(states.map((row) => row.state));
64:   } catch (err) {
65:     next(err);
66:   }
67: });
68: 
69: /**
70:  * GET /api/breweries/filters/types
71:  * Returns distinct list of brewery types for filter dropdowns.
72:  */
73: router.get('/filters/types', (req, res, next) => {
74:   try {
75:     const types = db.prepare('SELECT DISTINCT type FROM breweries WHERE type IS NOT NULL ORDER BY type ASC').all();
76:     res.json(types.map((row) => row.type));
77:   } catch (err) {
78:     next(err);
79:   }
80: });
81: 
82: /**
83:  * GET /api/breweries
84:  * Query: ?city= ?state= ?type= ?country= ?q= ?page= ?limit=
85:  */
86: router.get('/', (req, res, next) => {
87:   try {
88:     const page = Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.page, 10) || 1);
89:     const limit = Math.min(100, Math.max(1, parseInt(req.query.limit, 10) || 20));
90:     const offset = (page - 1) * limit;
91: 
92:     const { clause, params } = buildBreweryFilters(req.query);
93: 
94:     const { total } = db
95:       .prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM breweries ${clause}`)
96:       .get(...params);
97: 
98:     const rows = db
99:       .prepare(
100:         `SELECT * FROM breweries ${clause} ORDER BY name ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`
101:       )
102:       .all(...params, limit, offset);
103: 
104:     res.json({
105:       data: rows,
106:       page,
107:       limit,
108:       total,
109:       totalPages: Math.ceil(total / limit),
110:     });
111:   } catch (err) {
112:     next(err);
113:   }
114: });
115: 
116: /**
117:  * GET /api/breweries/:id
118:  */
119: router.get('/:id', (req, res, next) => {
120:   try {
121:     const brewery = db
122:       .prepare('SELECT * FROM breweries WHERE id = ?')
123:       .get(req.params.id);
124:     if (!brewery) {
125:       const err = new Error('Brewery not found');
126:       err.status = 404;
127:       throw err;
128:     }
129:     res.json({ brewery });
130:   } catch (err) {
131:     next(err);
132:   }
133: });
134: 
135: /**
136:  * POST /api/breweries
137:  * Protected: any authenticated user may add a brewery (admin-style in demo).
138:  * Body mirrors the breweries columns.
139:  */
140: router.post('/', auth, (req, res, next) => {
141:   try {
142:     const { name, address, city, state, country, lat, lng, website, phone, description, type } = req.body;
143:     if (!name) {
144:       const err = new Error('Brewery name is required');
145:       err.status = 400;
146:       throw err;
147:     }
148: 
149:     const info = db
150:       .prepare(
151:         `INSERT INTO breweries (name, address, city, state, country, lat, lng, website, phone, description, type)
152:          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
153:       )
154:       .run(
155:         name,
156:         address || null,
157:         city || null,
158:         state || null,
159:         country || 'USA',
160:         lat != null ? Number(lat) : null,
161:         lng != null ? Number(lng) : null,
162:         website || null,
163:         phone || null,
164:         description || null,
165:         type || null
166:       );
167: 
168:     const brewery = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM breweries WHERE id = ?').get(info.lastInsertRowid);
169:     res.status(201).json({ brewery });
170:   } catch (err) {
171:     next(err);
172:   }
173: });
174: 
175: /**
176:  * GET /api/breweries/country/:countryName
177:  * Proxy to Open Brewery DB for international brewery data.
178:  * Normalizes the response to match our schema.
179:  */
180: router.get('/country/:countryName', async (req, res, next) => {
181:   try {
182:     const { countryName } = req.params;
183:     const page = parseInt(req.query.page, 10) || 1;
184:     const perPage = parseInt(req.query.per_page, 10) || 50;
185: 
186:     const url = `https://api.openbrewerydb.org/v1/breweries?by_country=${encodeURIComponent(countryName)}&page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`;
187:     const response = await fetch(url);
188: 
189:     if (!response.ok) {
190:       const err = new Error(`Open Brewery DB proxy failed: ${response.statusText}`);
191:       err.status = response.status;
192:       throw err;
193:     }
194: 
195:     const data = await response.json();
196: 
197:     const normalized = data.map((b) => ({
198:       id: b.id || null,
199:       name: b.name || null,
200:       address: b.address_1 || null,
201:       city: b.city || null,
202:       state: b.state_province || b.state || null,
203:       country: b.country || null,
204:       lat: b.latitude != null ? Number(b.latitude) : null,
205:       lng: b.longitude != null ? Number(b.longitude) : null,
206:       website: b.website_url || null,
207:       phone: b.phone || null,
208:       description: null,
209:       type: b.brewery_type ? b.brewery_type.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + b.brewery_type.slice(1) : null,
210:       isExternal: true,
211:     }));
212: 
213:     res.json({
214:       data: normalized,
215:       page,
216:       perPage,
217:       total: normalized.length,
218:       totalPages: 1,
219:       source: 'Open Brewery DB',
220:     });
221:   } catch (err) {
222:     next(err);
223:   }
224: });
225: 
226: module.exports = router;

(End of file - total 226 lines)
</content>

86:     ).run(userId, challengeId);

1: 'use strict';
2: 
3: const express = require('express');
4: const { db } = require('../database/db');
5: const { auth } = require('../middleware/auth');
6: 
7: const router = express.Router();
8: 
9: /**
10:  * GET /api/reviews/brewery/:breweryId
11:  * Public list of reviews for a brewery, newest first, with author names.
12:  */
13: router.get('/brewery/:breweryId', (req, res, next) => {
14:   try {
15:     const brewery = db
16:       .prepare('SELECT id FROM breweries WHERE id = ?')
17:       .get(req.params.breweryId);
18:     if (!brewery) {
19:       const err = new Error('Brewery not found');
20:       err.status = 404;
21:       throw err;
22:     }
23: 
24:     const rows = db
25:       .prepare(
26:         `SELECT r.id, r.rating, r.comment, r.created_at, u.name AS author_name, u.id AS author_id
27:          FROM reviews r
28:          JOIN users u ON u.id = r.user_id
29:          WHERE r.brewery_id = ?
30:          ORDER BY r.created_at DESC`
31:       )
32:       .all(req.params.breweryId);
33: 
34:     // Aggregate average rating alongside the list.
35:     const { avg, count } = db
36:       .prepare(
37:         'SELECT AVG(rating) AS avg, COUNT(*) AS count FROM reviews WHERE brewery_id = ?'
38:       )
39:       .get(req.params.breweryId);
40: 
41:     res.json({
42:       data: rows,
43:       averageRating: avg != null ? Number(avg.toFixed(2)) : null,
44:       count,
45:     });
46:   } catch (err) {
47:     next(err);
48:   }
49: });
50: 
51: /**
52:  * POST /api/reviews
53:  * Protected: one review per user per brewery (UNIQUE constraint enforces it).
54:  * Body: { brewery_id, rating, comment? }
55:  */
56: router.post('/', auth, (req, res, next) => {
57:   try {
58:     const { brewery_id, rating, comment } = req.body;
59:     if (!brewery_id || rating == null) {
60:       const err = new Error('brewery_id and rating are required');
61:       err.status = 400;
62:       throw err;
63:     }
64: 
65:     const numericRating = Number(rating);
66:     if (!Number.isInteger(numericRating) || numericRating < 1 || numericRating > 5) {
67:       const err = new Error('Rating must be an integer between 1 and 5');
68:       err.status = 400;
69:       throw err;
70:     }
71: 
72:     const brewery = db.prepare('SELECT id FROM breweries WHERE id = ?').get(brewery_id);
73:     if (!brewery) {
74:       const err = new Error('Brewery not found');
75:       err.status = 404;
76:       throw err;
77:     }
78: 
79:     const info = db
80:       .prepare(
81:         'INSERT INTO reviews (user_id, brewery_id, rating, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)'
82:       )
83:       .run(req.user.id, brewery_id, numericRating, comment || null);
84: 
85:     const review = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE id = ?').get(info.lastInsertRowid);
86:     res.status(201).json({ review });
87:   } catch (err) {
88:     // UNIQUE(user_id, brewery_id) violation -> already reviewed.
89:     if (err.code === 'SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE') {
90:       err.status = 409;
91:       err.message = 'You have already reviewed this brewery';
92:     }
93:     next(err);
94:   }
95: });
96: 
97: module.exports = router;

(End of file - total 97 lines)

## Assistant (Orchestrator · Auto Free · 10.5s)

_Thinking:_

Now I have a complete picture of all the database code. I need to:

1. Create a new database abstraction layer using `pg` (Postgres client) instead of `better-sqlite3`
2. Convert schema.sql from SQLite to Postgres syntax
3. Convert seed.sql from SQLite to Postgres syntax
4. Update all route files to use the new Postgres-based db module

Key differences between SQLite and Postgres:
- `AUTOINCREMENT` → `SERIAL PRIMARY KEY` or `GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY`
- `TEXT` → `TEXT` (same)
- `REAL` → `REAL` (same)
- `INTEGER` → `INTEGER` (same)
- `DEFAULT (datetime('now'))` → `DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`
- `INSERT OR IGNORE` → `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` or use `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING`
- `lastInsertRowid` → use `RETURNING id`
- `changes` property → use `rowCount` in pg
- `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE` error code → Postgres uses `23505` for unique violations
- `COALESCE(?, column)` in UPDATE → same syntax works
- `RANDOM()` → `RANDOM()` (same in Postgres)

Let me also check the neon-postgres skill since the user mentioned it explicitly.

Let me plan the changes carefully:

**schema.sql changes:**
- `INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT` → `SERIAL PRIMARY KEY`
- `DEFAULT (datetime('now'))` → `DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`
- `CHECK (rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5)` → same
- `UNIQUE (user_id, brewery_id)` → same
- Indexes: same syntax

**seed.sql changes:**
- `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO breweries (id, ...) VALUES (...)` → `INSERT INTO breweries (id, ...) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING`

**db.js changes:**
- Replace better-sqlite3 with pg Pool
- Use connection pooling for serverless
- Handle schema initialization differently (run DDL via a connection)
- Keep seed logic but adapt for Postgres

**Route changes needed:**
- `info.lastInsertRowid` → use `RETURNING id` in INSERT queries
- `info.changes` → `result.rowCount` in pg
- `err.code === 'SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE'` → `err.code === '23505'`
- `datetime('now')` → `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` or `now()`
- `COALESCE(?, title)` → `COALESCE($1, title)` - parameter style changes

Actually, since the routes use `?` placeholders for SQLite, I need to change them to `$1, $2, ...` for Postgres. The `pg` package uses `$1, $2, ...` style parameters.

Wait - I could use a helper to convert `?` to `$n` style, but that adds complexity. It's cleaner to just update all the queries.

Let me think about the best approach. I'll create a `db.js` that wraps `pg` and provides similar methods to `better-sqlite3` to minimize changes in the route files.

This way, the route files need minimal changes - mainly just updating `lastInsertRowid` and `changes` references.

**Tool: task**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "description": "Create Postgres db abstraction layer",
  "prompt": "Create a new database abstraction layer for the Beer Road Save project at `/home/anton/projects/beer-road/server/database/db.js` that uses PostgreSQL via the `pg` package while keeping an API mostly compatible with `better-sqlite3` to minimize changes to route files.\n\n**Context:**\n- The project currently uses `better-sqlite3` with a `db.prepare(sql).get(...params)`, `db.prepare(sql).all(...params)`, `db.prepare(sql).run(...params)` pattern.\n- Route files expect `result.lastInsertRowid` after INSERTs, and `result.changes` to check affected rows.\n- We need a drop-in replacement that converts `?` placeholders to `$1, $2, ...` for Postgres.\n\n**Create the file with this exact content:**\n\n```js\n'use strict';\n\nconst { Pool } = require('pg');\n\n// Connection pooling for serverless / Vercel.\n// DATABASE_URL should be set (e.g. from Neon).\nconst connectionString = process.env.DATABASE_URL;\n\nif (!connectionString) {\n  console.warn('[db] DATABASE_URL not set. Database operations will fail.');\n}\n\nconst pool = new Pool({\n  connectionString,\n  ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false },\n  max: 5,\n  idleTimeoutMillis: 30000,\n  connectionTimeoutMillis: 10000,\n});\n\n/**\n * Convert SQLite-style ? placeholders to Postgres $1, $2, ... style.\n */\nfunction convertPlaceholders(sql) {\n  let index = 1;\n  return sql.replace(/\\?/g, () => `$${index++}`);\n}\n\n/**\n * Wrap a SQL statement so it can be executed with .get(), .all(), or .run().\n */\nfunction prepare(sql) {\n  const pgSql = convertPlaceholders(sql);\n\n  function get(...params) {\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      pool.query(pgSql, params, (err, result) => {\n        if (err) return reject(err);\n        resolve(result.rows[0] || null);\n      });\n    });\n  }\n\n  function all(...params) {\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      pool.query(pgSql, params, (err, result) => {\n        if (err) return reject(err);\n        resolve(result.rows);\n      });\n    });\n  }\n\n  function run(...params) {\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      pool.query(pgSql, params, (err, result) => {\n        if (err) return reject(err);\n        resolve({\n          lastInsertRowid: result.rows[0]?.id || 0,\n          changes: result.rowCount,\n        });\n      });\n    });\n  }\n\n  return { get, all, run };\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute raw SQL (for DDL like CREATE TABLE).\n */\nfunction exec(sql) {\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    pool.query(sql, (err) => {\n      if (err) return reject(err);\n      resolve();\n    });\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Initialize database schema and seed data.\n * Safe to call multiple times (idempotent).\n */\nasync function initializeDatabase() {\n  const schema = await fs.readFile(path.join(__dirname, 'schema.sql'), 'utf-8');\n  await exec(schema);\n  await seedIfEmpty();\n  return pool;\n}\n\n/**\n * Insert seed data only when tables are empty.\n */\nasync function seedIfEmpty() {\n  const result = await pool.query('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM breweries');\n  const breweryCount = parseInt(result.rows[0]?.count || '0', 10);\n\n  if (breweryCount > 0) return;\n\n  const seed = await fs.readFile(path.join(__dirname, 'seed.sql'), 'utf-8');\n  await exec(seed);\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { pool, prepare, exec, initializeDatabase, seedIfEmpty };\n```\n\n**Important:** The file imports `fs` and `path` at the top. Make sure to add those requires:\n```js\nconst fs = require('fs');\nconst path = require('path');\n```\n\nAlso add a helper for the Postgres UNIQUE violation error code that route files currently check:\n```js\n// Error code for unique constraint violations in Postgres\nconst PG_UNIQUE_VIOLATION = '23505';\n```\n\nReturn the full file content.",
  "subagent_type": "general"
}
```

The new db.js looks good. Now I need to:

This is a lot of work. Let me think about the minimal changes needed.

The route files currently use:
```js

```

This is synchronous in SQLite but will be async in Postgres. I need to change all route handlers to async.

Also, `info.lastInsertRowid` needs to work - my wrapper returns it via `RETURNING id`.

For `result.changes`, my wrapper returns `rowCount`.

For `err.code === 'SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE'`, I need to change to `err.code === PG_UNIQUE_VIOLATION`.

Let me now create the schema.sql, seed.sql, and update all route files.

For schema.sql, changes needed:
- `INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT` → `SERIAL PRIMARY KEY`
- `DEFAULT (datetime('now'))` → `DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`
- `UNIQUE (user_id, brewery_id)` → same
- Indexes: same

For seed.sql:
- `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ... (id, ...) VALUES (...)` → `INSERT INTO ... (id, ...) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING`

Wait, I also need to think about the `id` columns. In SQLite, `AUTOINCREMENT` ensures IDs are generated sequentially. In Postgres with `SERIAL`, the same happens. But in the seed data, we're explicitly inserting IDs. If the tables are empty, the IDs will be inserted. If they're not empty, `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING` will skip them.

Actually, for Postgres `SERIAL PRIMARY KEY`, if we explicitly insert IDs, they should work fine as long as we use `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING`.

Let me proceed with updating all files. I'll do this in batches to be efficient.

{
  "description": "Update server for Postgres",
  "prompt": "Update the Beer Road Save server files to work with PostgreSQL.\n\n**Step 1: Update `/home/anton/projects/beer-road/server/package.json`**\n\nReplace the dependencies block. Current:\n```json\n  \"dependencies\": {\n    \"bcrypt\": \"^5.1.1\",\n    \"better-sqlite3\": \"^11.3.0\",\n    \"cors\": \"^2.8.5\",\n    \"express\": \"^4.19.2\",\n    \"helmet\": \"^7.1.0\",\n    \"jsonwebtoken\": \"^9.0.2\",\n    \"serverless-http\": \"^3.0.0\"\n  },\n```\n\nChange to:\n```json\n  \"dependencies\": {\n    \"bcrypt\": \"^5.1.1\",\n    \"cors\": \"^2.8.5\",\n    \"express\": \"^4.19.2\",\n    \"helmet\": \"^7.1.0\",\n    \"jsonwebtoken\": \"^9.0.2\",\n    \"pg\": \"^8.11.0\"\n  },\n```\n\n**Step 2: Update `/home/anton/projects/beer-road/server/server.js`**\n\nMake `initializeDatabase()` async. Change:\n```js\n// Create tables and load seed data before handling requests.\ninitializeDatabase();\n```\n\nTo:\n```js\n// Create tables and load seed data before handling requests.\nawait initializeDatabase();\n```\n\nAlso wrap the app.listen in an async IIFE or make the file handle async startup. The cleanest way:\n\nReplace the bottom of the file (from `initializeDatabase();` to `module.exports = app;`) with:\n\n```js\n// Create tables and load seed data before handling requests.\nawait initializeDatabase();\n\napp.listen(PORT, () => {\n  console.log(`Beer Road Save API listening on http://localhost:${PORT}`);\n});\n\nmodule.exports = app;\n```\n\nSince `initializeDatabase` is now async, we need to make the top-level code handle that. The simplest approach is to wrap in an async function:\n\nReplace the entire file content with:\n\n```js\n'use strict';\n\nconst express = require('express');\nconst cors = require('cors');\nconst helmet = require('helmet');\nconst path = require('path');\n\nconst { initializeDatabase } = require('./database/db');\nconst authRoutes = require('./routes/auth');\nconst breweryRoutes = require('./routes/breweries');\nconst tripRoutes = require('./routes/trips');\nconst favoriteRoutes = require('./routes/favorites');\nconst reviewRoutes = require('./routes/reviews');\nconst beerRoutes = require('./routes/beers');\nconst challengeRoutes = require('./routes/challenges');\nconst { notFoundHandler, errorHandler } = require('./middleware/errorHandler');\n\nconst PORT = process.env.PORT || 3001;\n// Frontend dev server (Vite default).\nconst CLIENT_ORIGIN = process.env.CLIENT_ORIGIN || 'http://localhost:5173';\n\nconst app = express();\n\n// --- Core middleware ---\napp.use(helmet());\napp.use(cors({ origin: CLIENT_ORIGIN }));\napp.use(express.json());\napp.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true }));\n\n// --- Health check ---\napp.get('/api/health', (req, res) => {\n  res.json({ status: 'ok', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() });\n});\n\n// --- API routes ---\napp.use('/api/auth', authRoutes);\napp.use('/api/breweries', breweryRoutes);\napp.use('/api/trips', tripRoutes);\napp.use('/api/favorites', favoriteRoutes);\napp.use('/api/reviews', reviewRoutes);\napp.use('/api/beers', beerRoutes);\napp.use('/api/challenges', challengeRoutes);\n\n// Serve a basic static client build if present (optional production asset).\nconst publicDir = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'public');\napp.use(express.static(publicDir));\n\n// --- Error handling (must be last) ---\napp.use(notFoundHandler);\napp.use(errorHandler);\n\n// Initialize DB then start server.\nasync function start() {\n  try {\n    await initializeDatabase();\n    app.listen(PORT, () => {\n      console.log(`Beer Road Save API listening on http://localhost:${PORT}`);\n    });\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error('Failed to start server:', err);\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nstart();\n\nmodule.exports = app;\n```\n\n**Step 3: Update `/home/anton/projects/beer-road/api/index.js`**\n\nThe api/index.js needs to wait for the Express app to be ready. Since server.js now exports `app` immediately but initializes DB asynchronously, we need the wrapper to handle cold starts properly.\n\nReplace the content with:\n\n```js\nconst serverless = require('serverless-http');\nconst { app } = require('../server/server');\n\n// Vercel serverless handler\nmodule.exports = serverless(app);\n\n// Support direct local execution\nif (require.main === module) {\n  const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3001;\n  app.listen(PORT, () => {\n    console.log(`Beer Road Save API listening on http://localhost:${PORT}`);\n  });\n}\n```\n\nReturn confirmation all 3 files are updated.",
  "subagent_type": "general"
}

```
<task id="ses_09af55366ffeZdRHFCyus1wjXU" state="completed">
<task_result>
All 3 files updated:
- `server/package.json` — swapped `better-sqlite3` + `serverless-http` for `pg`.
- `server/server.js` — `initializeDatabase()` now runs async inside a `start()` function before `app.listen`.
- `api/index.js` — requires `serverless(app)` and supports direct local execution.

One conflict to flag: `api/index.js` still `require('serverless-http')`, but Step 1 removed `serverless-http` from dependencies. The server will fail to import unless you either keep `serverless-http` in deps or switch `api/index.js` to use `app` directly (e.g. via `vercel` Node runtime). Also note `./database/db` must be implemented with `pg` for these to actually run.
</task_result>
</task>

_Thinking:_

The user wants me to use Neon Postgres. I need to add serverless-http back to the dependencies since api/index.js still uses it. Then I need to update all route files to work with the async Postgres database layer.

Let me:
1. Fix package.json (add serverless-http back)
2. Update all route files to use async db calls

This is a massive change across 7 route files. Each file needs:
- All `db.prepare(...).get(...)` → `await db.prepare(...).get(...)`
- All `db.prepare(...).all(...)` → `await db.prepare(...).all(...)`
- All `db.prepare(...).run(...)` → `await db.prepare(...).run(...)`
- Route handler functions need to be `async`
- `err.code === 'SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE'` → `err.code === '23505'` or use the PG_UNIQUE_VIOLATION constant
- Any `datetime('now')` in SQL → `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` or `now()`
- Any `COALESCE(?, title)` patterns should still work since our wrapper converts `?` to `$1, $2, ...`

Actually wait - `COALESCE(?, title)` in Postgres works with parameterized queries. The `?` gets converted to `$1`, so `COALESCE($1, title)` is valid Postgres.

Also `RANDOM()` works in both SQLite and Postgres.

Let me check which SQL features need changing:
- `datetime('now')` → `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` - used in challenges.js and trips.js
- `info.lastInsertRowid` → wrapper handles this
- `result.changes` → wrapper handles this via rowCount

Let me create a big task to update all route files at once.

1: 'use strict';
2: 
3: const express = require('express');
4: const bcrypt = require('bcrypt');
5: const { db } = require('../database/db');
6: const { auth, generateToken } = require('../middleware/auth');
7: 
8: const router = express.Router();
9: 
10: /**
11:  * POST /api/auth/register
12:  * Body: { email, password, name }
13:  */
14: router.post('/register', async (req, res, next) => {
15:   try {
16:     const { email, password, name } = req.body;
17:     if (!email || !password) {
18:       const err = new Error('Email and password are required');
19:       err.status = 400;
20:       throw err;
21:     }
22:     if (String(password).length < 6) {
23:       const err = new Error('Password must be at least 6 characters');
24:       err.status = 400;
25:       throw err;
26:     }
27: 
28:     const normalizedEmail = String(email).toLowerCase().trim();
29:     const existing = await db.prepare('SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?').get(normalizedEmail);
30:     if (existing) {
31:       const err = new Error('Email already registered');
32:       err.status = 409;
33:       throw err;
34:     }
35: 
36:     const passwordHash = bcrypt.hashSync(password, 10);
37:     const info = await db
38:       .prepare('INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)')
39:       .run(normalizedEmail, passwordHash, name ? String(name).trim() : null);
40: 
41:     const user = await db
42:       .prepare('SELECT id, email, name, created_at FROM users WHERE id = ?')
43:       .get(info.lastInsertRowid);
44: 
45:     res.status(201).json({ token: generateToken(user), user });
46:   } catch (err) {
47:     next(err);
48:   }
49: });
50: 
51: /**
52:  * POST /api/auth/login
53:  * Body: { email, password }
54:  */
55: router.post('/login', async (req, res, next) => {
56:   try {
57:     const { email, password } = req.body;
58:     if (!email || !password) {
59:       const err = new Error('Email and password are required');
60:       err.status = 400;
61:       throw err;
62:     }
63: 
64:     const user = await db
65:       .prepare('SELECT id, email, name, password_hash FROM users WHERE email = ?')
66:       .get(String(email).toLowerCase().trim());
67:     if (!user || !bcrypt.compareSync(password, user.password_hash)) {
68:       const err = new Error('Invalid credentials');
69:       err.status = 401;
70:       throw err;
71:     }
72: 
73:     const safeUser = { id: user.id, email: user.email, name: user.name };
74:     res.json({ token: generateToken(safeUser), user: safeUser });
75:   } catch (err) {
76:     next(err);
77:   }
78: });
79:
